content.text:
MEMORY-ONLY MAINTENANCE TASK

                This is a private memory pipeline call, not a normal assistant reply.
                Do not answer as a chat assistant.
                Do not call tools.
                Execute only the maintenance stage below.

                Output contract:
                Return exactly one valid JSON object matching the response schema.
                No prose, no markdown fences, no explanation.

                        You are NoteConsolidator v2 for a long-term AI agent memory system.

        Goal:
        Review selected notes and decide whether any of them should now produce durable structured memory.

        Current time: <instant>
        Timezone: Asia/Jerusalem
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Selected notes:
        - id=hot-path:note:<0>; type=LESSON; status=ACTIVE; maturity=FRESH; importance=4; confidence=0.98; title=SourceHydrator should use bounded quote hydration on evidence fallback; summary=In Memory MVP's SourceHydrator flow, when factual answers need raw evidence after interpreted claims are retrieved, fetch only a small bounded set of source quotes and only on explicit evidence fallback requests. This keeps answers grounded while avoiding prompt bloat from dumping all raw sources.; scope=Applies to Memory MVP SourceHydrator design for source evidence hydration behavior.; anchor=entity:<0>; entities=entity:<1>;entity:<2>;project:<1>; evidence=chat:<0> for source evidence hydration, prefer bounded quote hydration over dumping all sources into the runtime prompt.; candidate_claims=["Prefer bounded quote hydration over dumping all sources into runtime prompt for source evidence hydration.","Fetch a small bounded set of source quotes only when evidence fallback is requested."]
- id=hot-path:note:<1>; type=LESSON; status=ACTIVE; maturity=FRESH; importance=4; confidence=0.96; title=Use model-based verification for ambiguous no-memory recall decisions; summary=In Memory MVP's NoMemoryVerifier flow, when ReadTimeRetrievalPlanner returns need_memory=false on ambiguous recall questions, run a short model-based verification call before accepting the no-memory plan, and avoid hard-coded substring heuristics to reduce brittle lexical behavior while preserving recall-intent detection.; scope=Applies to Memory MVP retrieval-planning and NoMemoryVerifier decision logic for ambiguous recall cases.; anchor=entity:<3>; entities=entity:<4>;entity:<5>;entity:<6>;entity:<7>; evidence=chat:<1> for ambiguous no-memory recall decisions, prefer a cheap model-based verifier over substring matching.; candidate_claims=[]

        Entity catalog:
        - id=entity:<0>; type=CONCEPT; name=SourceHydrator; summary=Concept named SourceHydrator.
- id=entity:<8>; type=CONCEPT; name=need_memory=false; summary=Concept named need_memory=false.
- id=entity:<3>; type=PROJECT; name=Memory MVP; summary=Project named Memory MVP.
- id=project:<0>; type=PROJECT; name=project:<0>; summary=Project named project:<2>
- id=entity:<9>; type=SERVICE; name=NoMemoryVerifier; summary=Service named NoMemoryVerifier.
- id=entity:<10>; type=SERVICE; name=ReadTimeRetrievalPlanner; summary=Service named ReadTimeRetrievalPlanner.

        Related active claims:
        none

        Related active notes:
        none

        Related tasks:
        none

        Existing profile:
        none

        Return JSON:
        {
          "claim_candidates": [],
          "task_actions": [],
          "profile_patch": null,
          "episode_candidates": [],
          "note_actions": [
            {
              "note_id": "note-id",
              "action": "keep_active | mark_resolved | mark_stale | supersede | mark_consolidated",
              "reason": "short explanation"
            }
          ],
          "summary": "short summary"
        }

        Rules:
        - Consolidate only when the signal is strong enough.
        - Do not force every note into a claim.
        - Promote to claim when the meaning is stable, queryable, and precise enough.
        - Promote to task only when future action is clearly required and already represented by the selected note.
        - Return one episode_candidate for each selected LESSON note that clearly contains a reusable situation-action-result-lesson pattern.
        - Do not collapse several unrelated selected lessons into one episode_candidate.
        - For episode_candidates, owner_entity_id should be the most specific component/service/person/concept the lesson applies to. Use the project entity only when no more specific owner exists.
        - If a selected note is a useful stable rule but not reusable experience, promote it to claim without creating an episode_candidate.
        - Write claim text, task text, profile text, episode text, and reasons in English.
        - Preserve proper names, component names, repo names, exact identifiers, dates, commands, and quoted terms.
        - Every claim candidate must include origin_note_id from Selected notes.
        - Use only entity ids from Entity catalog.
        - If no entity is better, use the selected note anchor entity.
        - Use object_entity_id for entity objects and object_value_json for scalar/string objects, never both.
        - evidence_quote may be null for derived memory; provenance will use the origin note evidence refs.
        - Existing claims and tasks are dedup/context only.
        - If a selected note already has a matching active claim or task, prefer note_actions with mark_consolidated or keep_active instead of duplicating memory.
        - Use mark_consolidated only when durable memory was created or already exists.
        - Use keep_active for unresolved rationale, hypotheses, weak ideas, or notes that remain useful as notes.
        - Return valid JSON only.

responseFormat.description: Consolidate mature notes into durable memory objects and note lifecycle actions.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_consolidator

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

strategy.","importance":4,"normalized_text":"SourceHydrator should use bounded quote hydration instead of dumping all raw sources into the runtime prompt.","object_entity_id":null,"object_value_json":"Prefer bounded quote hydration over dumping all sources into the runtime prompt for source evidence hydration.","origin_note_id":"hot-path:note:<0>","predicate":"guideline","qualifiers_json":{},"reason":"Stable architectural rule with clear component scope and durable reuse value.","scope_json":{"basis":"explicit","kind":"project","text":"Memory MVP"},"subject_entity_id":"entity:<0>","valid_from":null,"valid_to":null},{"confidence":0.97,"context_text":"Evidence fallback path in Memory MVP factual answering.","evidence_kind":"summarized","evidence_quote":"Fetch a small bounded set of source quotes only when evidence fallback is requested.","evidence_reason":"Candidate claim and note summary explicitly describe the fallback trigger and bounded behavior.","importance":4,"normalized_text":"SourceHydrator should fetch a small bounded quote set only on explicit evidence fallback requests.","object_entity_id":null,"object_value_json":"Fetch only a small bounded set of source quotes, and only when explicit evidence fallback is requested.","origin_note_id":"hot-path:note:<0>","predicate":"evidence_fallback_policy","qualifiers_json":{},"reason":"Precise conditional policy suitable for retrieval and enforcement.","scope_json":{"basis":"explicit","kind":"project","text":"Memory MVP"},"subject_entity_id":"entity:<0>","valid_from":null,"valid_to":null},{"confidence":0.96,"context_text":"Memory MVP retrieval-planning and NoMemoryVerifier logic.","evidence_kind":"direct","evidence_quote":"Lesson: for ambiguous no-memory recall decisions, prefer a cheap model-based verifier over substring matching.","evidence_reason":"Direct lesson text specifies preferred verifier strategy.","importance":4,"normalized_text":"NoMemoryVerifier should use a cheap model-based verifier for ambiguous need_memory=false recall decisions.","object_entity_id":null,"object_value_json":"For ambiguous recall cases where ReadTimeRetrievalPlanner returns need_memory=false, run a short model-based verification call before accepting the no-memory plan.","origin_note_id":"hot-path:note:<1>","predicate":"verification_strategy","qualifiers_json":{},"reason":"Clear and reusable decision rule tied to a specific service and condition.","scope_json":{"basis":"explicit","kind":"project","text":"Memory MVP"},"subject_entity_id":"entity:<9>","valid_from":null,"valid_to":null},{"confidence":0.94,"context_text":"Ambiguous recall decision handling in Memory MVP.","evidence_kind":"derived_from_note","evidence_quote":null,"evidence_reason":"Derived from the note summary that explicitly warns against hard-coded substring heuristics.","importance":3,"normalized_text":"NoMemoryVerifier should avoid brittle substring heuristics in ambiguous recall-intent decisions.","object_entity_id":null,"object_value_json":"Avoid hard-coded substring heuristics for ambiguous no-memory recall-intent detection.","origin_note_id":"hot-path:note:<1>","predicate":"avoid_method","qualifiers_json":{},"reason":"Negative constraint complements the preferred strategy and is stable enough for durable memory.","scope_json":{"basis":"explicit","kind":"project","text":"Memory MVP"},"subject_entity_id":"entity:<9>","valid_from":null,"valid_to":null}],"episode_candidates":[{"action":"Adjusted SourceHydrator to perform bounded quote hydration and trigger it only on explicit evidence fallback requests.","lesson":"Use bounded, on-demand evidence quote hydration rather than full source dumps in evidence fallback paths.","origin_note_id":"hot-path:note:<0>","owner_entity_id":"entity:<0>","reason":"Contains clear situation-action-result pattern and reusable implementation lesson.","result":"Responses stay grounded with source evidence while avoiding runtime prompt overload from dumping all raw sources.","situation":"In Memory MVP, factual answers sometimes required raw evidence after interpreted claims were retrieved, risking prompt bloat if all sources were injected.","success_score":0.92,"tags":["memory-mvp","sourcehydrator","evidence","prompt-size","retrieval"]},{"action":"Introduced a short model-based verification step in NoMemoryVerifier before accepting no-memory plans, replacing substring-based heuristics for ambiguous cases.","lesson":"For ambiguous no-memory recall decisions, prefer cheap model-based verification over hard-coded substring matching.","origin_note_id":"hot-path:note:<1>","owner_entity_id":"entity:<9>","reason":"Lesson is a concrete reusable decision pattern with explicit tradeoff and outcome.","result":"Reduced brittle lexical behavior while preserving recall-intent detection quality.","situation":"In Memory MVP, ambiguous recall questions could be misclassified when ReadTimeRetrievalPlanner returned need_memory=false based on brittle lexical cues.","success_score":0.9,"tags":["memory-mvp","nomemoryverifier","retrieval-planner","verification","classification"]}],"note_actions":[{"action":"mark_consolidated","note_id":"hot-path:note:<0>","reason":"Promoted stable SourceHydrator policies to durable claims and captured reusable episode."},{"action":"mark_consolidated","note_id":"hot-path:note:<1>","reason":"Promoted stable NoMemoryVerifier decision rules to durable claims and captured reusable episode."}],"profile_patch":null,"summary":"Consolidated both fresh LESSON notes into durable project-scoped claims and created one reusable episode per lesson; no tasks or profile updates needed.","task_actions":[]}
